title: SPARQL result format JSON types bnodes as literal or uri
description:
When querying a graph containing blank nodes the JSON format will type them inconsistently as literal or uri as in the following snippet. You can reproduce this with the http://vocab.ub.uni-leipzig.de/bibrm/ model, which you can find in our github repo: https://github.com/ERM-HD/vocab.bibrm/blob/develop/bibrm.ttl
…
{ "resourceUri": { "type": "uri", "value": "_:node1" }  , "p": { "type": "uri", "value": "http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#first" }   , "o": { "type": "uri", "value": "http://vocab.ub.uni-leipzig.de/bibrm/Database" }},
{ "resourceUri": { "type": "uri", "value": "_:node1" }  , "p": { "type": "uri", "value": "http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#rest" }    , "o": { "type": "literal", "value": "_:node2" }},
{ "resourceUri": { "type": "uri", "value": "_:node2" }  , "p": { "type": "uri", "value": "http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#first" }   , "o": { "type": "uri", "value": "http://vocab.ub.uni-leipzig.de/bibrm/Ejournal" }},
{ "resourceUri": { "type": "uri", "value": "_:node2" }  , "p": { "type": "uri", "value": "http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#rest" }    , "o": { "type": "literal", "value": "_:node3" }},
{ "resourceUri": { "type": "uri", "value": "_:node3" }  , "p": { "type": "uri", "value": "http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#first" }   , "o": { "type": "uri", "value": "http://vocab.ub.uni-leipzig.de/bibrm/EjournalPackage" }},
…

The correct output would be { "type": "bnode", "value": "node2" } as I understand the SPARQL recommendation.
I ran
SELECT ?resourceUri ?p ?o {
    ?resourceUri ?p ?o
}

ans selected JSON as „Results Format“.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE bnodes (id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY, resourceUri VARCHAR(255), p VARCHAR(255), o VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO bnodes (resourceUri, p, o) VALUES ('_:node1', 'http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#first', 'http://vocab.ub.uni-leipzig.de/bibrm/Database');
INSERT INTO bnodes (resourceUri, p, o) VALUES ('_:node1', 'http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#rest', NULL);
SELECT resourceUri, p, IFNULL(o, 'NULL') AS o FROM bnodes WHERE resourceUri IS NOT NULL;
UPDATE bnodes SET o = '_:node2' WHERE resourceUri = '_:node1' AND o IS NULL;
key_bug_features: Inconsistent typing of blank nodes in JSON output
